assertions.title: WelcomeScreen rendering/behavior is preserved
assertions.reason: The WelcomeScreen Jest suite passed 5/5, including the CTA press, matching accessibility label, header role, and no-PIN checks, and the source scan shows the preserved Secure by default / Own your identity / Connect anywhere feature rows remain rendered with the Get started anchor.

commands: rg -n -e 'Secure by default' -e 'Own your identity' -e 'Connect anywhere' -e 'Get started' -e 'accessibilityLabel="Get started"' -e 'accessibilityRole="header"' src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.tsx src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.test.tsx
testNames: renders the product value proposition
invokes the start callback
exposes the Get started CTA with a matching accessibility label
sets accessibilityRole="header" on the hero title
does not mention PIN in any feature-row copy

assertions.title: Settings — "Reset wallet" wipes biometric secret and returns to first launch
assertions.reason: The SettingsScreen suite passed 9/9 and proved the destructive confirmation copy stays biometric-specific, reset cancellation is inert, confirm triggers deleteSecret → teardown → session reset → hydrate in order, and the wallet-root alias is used. A targeted reset-flow Jest check also passed and showed the reset snapshot routes back to Welcome after the post-reset biometric re-probe.

commands: bun run test -- src/__tests__/cross-area/reset-and-restore-flow.test.tsx -t "reset clears all state"
testNames: shows a confirmation alert with biometric-referenced copy (no PIN) when Reset wallet is pressed
does NOT invoke any reset primitive when the user cancels the confirmation alert
calls NativeBiometricVault.deleteSecret + teardown + sessionStore.reset + hydrate in order when the user confirms
calls deleteSecret against the canonical wallet-root keystore alias
still triggers hydrate even if agentStore.reset() rejects (best-effort cleanup)
reset clears all state + a second first-launch produces a different DID

assertions.title: Settings — stale "Change PIN" row is removed
assertions.reason: The SettingsScreen suite passed the explicit no-PIN assertions, and the direct source scan returned no matches for either 'Change PIN' or standalone 'PIN' text in settings-screen.tsx.

testNames: does not render a "Change PIN" row
never renders the literal `PIN` anywhere on the settings tree
still renders the post-refactor Security rows (Lock wallet + Biometric unlock)

assertions.title: Accessibility anchors on all new screens
assertions.reason: All six referenced screen suites passed on the local Jest surface, and the combined anchor scan shows getByLabelText/getByRole('header') coverage plus matching accessibilityLabel/header-role implementation for Open Settings, Enable biometric unlock, Unlock with…, I’ve saved it, Restore wallet, and the preserved Get started CTA.

commands: rg -n -e 'Open Settings' -e 'Enable biometric unlock' -e 'Unlock with' -e 'I.u2019ve saved it|I.ve saved it' -e 'Restore wallet' -e 'getByRole\('header'\)' -e 'getByLabelText' -e 'accessibilityRole="header"' -e 'accessibilityLabel=' src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unavailable-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unavailable-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-setup-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-setup-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unlock-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unlock-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.tsx src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.test.tsx
testNames: exposes the Get started CTA with a matching accessibility label
renders an Open Settings button with accessibilityLabel
renders the "Enable biometric unlock" CTA with matching a11y label
renders a CTA whose label and a11y label both start with "Unlock with"
renders the confirm button with matching text and accessibilityLabel
exposes the recovery-restore-screen testID + "Restore wallet" CTA anchors

assertions.title: Stable string anchors for CI UI driver
assertions.reason: The anchor source/test scan shows all required literal anchors are present on their respective screens—Get started, Open Settings, Enable biometric unlock, Unlock with… prefix, I’ve saved it, and Restore wallet—and each corresponding Jest suite passed on the assigned local surface.

commands: rg -n -e 'Open Settings' -e 'Enable biometric unlock' -e 'Unlock with' -e 'I.u2019ve saved it|I.ve saved it' -e 'Restore wallet' -e 'Get started' src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unavailable-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unavailable-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-setup-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-setup-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unlock-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/biometric-unlock-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.tsx src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.tsx src/features/onboarding/screens/welcome-screen.test.tsx
testNames: exposes the Get started CTA with a matching accessibility label
renders an Open Settings button with accessibilityLabel
renders the "Enable biometric unlock" CTA with matching a11y label
renders a CTA whose label and a11y label both start with "Unlock with"
renders the confirm button with matching text and accessibilityLabel
exposes the recovery-restore-screen testID + "Restore wallet" CTA anchors

summary: Tested 5 assigned assertions on the jest-local surface: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Ran 7 targeted screen suites plus 1 reset-flow spot check (71 passing tests total) and saved all cited rg/Jest outputs under the welcome-settings-anchors evidence directory.
